title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Linux Stakeholder Mission
summary: An operations team shares scripts and reports but must prevent accidental cross-team writes. Create and defend ownership, mode, umask, group, process, signal, and recovery decisions. Planned artifact: Deliver Least-Privilege Shared Service Workspace with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
Interpret size, allocation, ownership, mode, inode, timestamps, and link count with stat and ls evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
